BLOCKER.description: Refuse-to-ship. Hard violation of an invariant. Must be fixed before output reaches the operator.
examples: Generating mode cell with no verbatim anchor and no breakthrough flag
Mutation gate bypass attempted (Edit/Write on brand JSON)

MAJOR.description: Surface to operator with explicit flag. Output may ship but operator must see the issue and choose to override.
examples: Voice consistency cross-block drift (persona varies > 1 step)
Emotional diversity collapse (top N angles all same emotional driver)

MINOR.description: Log to backlog. Do not surface to operator unless cumulative pattern detected. Cosmetic or stylistic deviation.
examples: Lexical register slightly off canonical brand voice
Cell sourcing tag missing but inferable from context

sourcing_tags.observed: Scraped literally from the source (web page, API response, customer voice). High confidence.
sourcing_tags.stated: Declared by the operator or an authoritative source. Authoritative confidence within scope.
sourcing_tags.structured: Computed by deterministic function over observed/stated inputs. Reproducible.
sourcing_tags.derived: Inferred by model reasoning over inputs. Hypothesis confidence — must be validated before treating as canonical.

canon_origin_tags.canon_ref: References a canonical literature ID (e.g. BA-002, INF-006, HOR-001, Porter, Christensen). Authoritative.
canon_origin_tags.derived: Skill-author derived with explicit reasoning. Lower trust than canon_ref. Must carry derivation rationale.

_change_log.change: Initial centralization. Previously scattered across analyze-copy SKILL.md, validate-resources, compatibility-rules.json. Operationalizes architectural invariants from internal canon.
